rulings.Case Title: PETER PEDRETTI et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Plaintiff Peter Pedretti For Failure To Prosecute Claim
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 18, 2016 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Honeywell International, Inc.'s motion to dismiss plaintiff Peter Pedretti for failure to prosecute is off calendar as moot. A dismissal as to Peter Pedretti was filed February 4, 2016. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. =(503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: OSCEOLA HEATH ET AL VS. 3M COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 18, 2016 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Astra Flooring Company's motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' strict liability cause of action and claim for punitive damages is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: OSCEOLA HEATH ET AL VS. 3M COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 18, 2016 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Fraser's Boiler Service, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden by demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, clear and convincing evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs failed to submit evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression. While plaintiffs do not need to prove their case for punitive damages at the summary adjudication stage, the Court takes the higher proof standard into consideration. (See American Airlines, Inc. v. Sheppard, Mullin, Richter & Hampton (2002) 96 Cal.App.4th 1017; Hoch v. Allied-Signal, Inc. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 48.) Defendant's objections to the transcript of Harry's Fraser's 1994 deposition attached to plaintiffs' Exhibit 1 are sustained on the grounds that the attached excerpts do not include the portions quoted in Bruce Fraser's deposition. Defendant's objection no. 2 (portions of Bruce Fraser's deposition) is overruled. Defendant's motion for summary judgment is off calendar at defendant's request. Defendant's motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' strict liability cause of action is off calendar as moot pursuant to the parties' representations that plaintiffs have agreed to dismiss that cause of action, although at this time the Court does not yet see a dismissal on file. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/GLW)
